title: Jerni
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Species |image=[[File:Jerni-DKD.png]] |name=Jerni
{{C|As depicted in Darth Vader's fake image tapes}} |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]]{{StoryCite|story=Dark Knight's Devilry|book=The Empire Strikes Back Monthly 153}} |class=[[Humanoid/Legends|Humanoid]] {{C|As depicted in [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] fake [[image tape]]s}} |subspecies= |races= |height= |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor=[[Color/Legends|Maroon]] {{C|As depicted in [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader's fake image tapes}} |haircolor= |feathers= |eyecolor= |distinctions= |lifespan= |origin=[[Jerne]] |habitat=[[Desert/Legends|Desert]] |diet= |language= }} {{Quote|It's something called the Eternity Crystal… We're not exactly sure how it works… But apparently it was the pinnacle of Jerni science. Some sort of machine that could wind time backwards…until it reached a critical point in history…and then time would start to flow forward again…but along a different path.|Luke Skywalker|}} The [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] '''Jerni''' were the original inhabitants of the ancient [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Desert/Legends|desert]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Jerne]], where they once ruled over a glorious empire before suffering extinction many eons before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. In a failed bid to capture [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] shortly after the [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destruction of Alderaan]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] tricked his nemesis into [[Mission to Jerne|chasing after]] the fabled [[Eternity Crystal]], a device allegedly crafted by the Jerni that promised to save [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] through the power of [[Time travel/Legends|time travel]]. ==Biology and appearance== The Jerni were once a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] that lived among the pale sands of their [[Desert/Legends|desert]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], [[Jerne]]. According to [[image tape]]s concocted as a hoax by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], the Jerni were [[Hair/Legends|hairless]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] with [[Color/Legends|maroon]] skin, oval heads, and wide [[Nose/Legends|nostrils]]. ==Society and culture== The Jerni civilization consisted of domed, [[Window/Legends|windowed]] structures of varying sizes. [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader's phony image tapes suggested a vast society spread out across innumerable such structures, with plentiful [[Plant/Legends|vegetation]] interspersed between them. ==History== {{Quote|Right from the time I had that 'alien' ship built…and concocted its phony image tapes with their lies about the Eternity Crystal.|Darth Vader gloats over his plan to capture Princess Leia Organa|}} [[File:JerniRuins-DKD.png|thumb|right|275px|Ancient Jerni ruins in the Jerne desert]] As the original inhabitants of the ancient [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Jerne system/Legends|Jerne system]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] Jerne, the Jerni controlled a once-great empire on their homeworld [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. However, the Jerni eventually became extinct, and only a few scattered ruins remained of their civilization by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Sometime after the [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destruction]] of the planet [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]],"[[Dark Knight's Devilry]]" takes place following the [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destruction of Alderaan]] and the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] while [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] still uses [[Anakin Skywalker's second lightsaber/Legends|Anakin Skywalker's lightsaber]], which he [[Duel on Cloud City/Legends|loses]] during the events of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']]. This [[time]] period corresponds to the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. during the Galactic Civil War, Darth Vader devised a scheme to capture [[Princess/Legends|Princess]]{{DB|character|leiaorganasolo|Solo, Princess Leia Organa}} [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] by using the promise of a great Jerni [[Scientist/Legends|scientific]] wonder as bait. Knowing that Organa would not pass up a chance to possibly save her beloved [[Mundicide|destroyed]] homeworld, Vader invented a hoax centered around the fabled [[Eternity Crystal]], a relic supposedly built by the Jerni that could [[Time travel/Legends|reverse time]] to a critical juncture in history, at which point time would then continue as normal but along a different path. Vader's plan included the creation of phony image tapes that depicted the Jerni harnessing the power of the Eternity Crystal to undo a destructive war and reclaim a period of peace and prosperity on their homeworld. The Dark Lord also had a sleek [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] built, complete with a falsified [[Ship's log|log]] stating that it had been built by the Jerni and had come from the planet Jerne, then sent it into the nearby void of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] for the Rebels to find. Sure enough, Vader's ruse [[Mission to Jerne|attracted]] Organa and her Rebel ally [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] to Jerne, believing that they could find the Eternity Crystal and use it to save Alderaan from its fate or even prevent the Empire from existing at all. However, Vader's plan ultimately failed, and both Rebels escaped from Jerne. ==Behind the scenes== The Jerni were created for the [[Star Wars comic books|comic story]] "[[Dark Knight's Devilry]]," which was written by [[Steve Moore]], illustrated by [[Alan Davis]], and originally published by [[Marvel Comics]] for the [[The Empire Strikes Back Monthly 153|153th issue]] of ''[[The Empire Strikes Back Monthly]]'' in [[January]] [[1982]].''[[Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: The Original Marvel Years Vol. 3]]'' "Dark Knight's Devilry" depicts the Jerni within the context of Darth Vader's phony image tapes, leaving the species' actual physical characteristics ambiguous. [[Dark Horse Comics]] republished "Dark Knight's Devilry" in the [[Classic Star Wars: Devilworlds 1|first issue]] of its ''[[Classic Star Wars: Devilworlds]]'' series,{{StoryCite|story=Dark Knight's Devilry|book=Classic Star Wars: Devilworlds 1}} which was released on [[August 21]], [[1996]].{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/53775/classic_star_wars_devilworlds_1996_1|text=Classic Star Wars: Devilworlds (1996) #1}} This reissue presented "Dark Knight's Devilry" for the first time in color by artist [[Matt Webb]] as the story was originally published in black and white in ''The Empire Strikes Back Monthly''.''[[Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space Volume 1]]'' ==Appearances== *{{StoryCite|story=Dark Knight's Devilry|book=The Empire Strikes Back Monthly 153|reprint=1}} {{1st}} {{C|Depicted in Darth Vader's fake image tapes}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Jerni}} *{{CSWECite|II||Jerne}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Jerni }} [[Category:Extinct sentient species]] [[Category:Outer Rim Territories sentient species]]
